$3); }\n\t\t;\n\nwindow_definition:\n\t\t\tColId AS window_specification\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tWindowDef  *n = $3;\n\n\t\t\t\t\tn->name = $1;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = n;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t;\n\nover_clause: OVER window_specification\n\t\t\t\t{ $$ = $2; }\n\t\t\t| OVER ColId\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tWindowDef  *n = makeNode(WindowDef);\n\n\t\t\t\t\tn->name = $2;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->refname = NULL;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->partitionClause = NIL;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->orderClause = NIL;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->frameOptions = FRAMEOPTION_DEFAULTS;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->startOffset = NULL;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->endOffset = NULL;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->location = @2;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = n;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| /*EMPTY*/\n\t\t\t\t{ $$ = NULL; }\n\t\t;\n\nwindow_specification: '(' opt_existing_window_name opt_partition_clause\n\t\t\t\t\t\topt_sort_clause opt_frame_clause ')'\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tWindowDef  *n = makeNode(WindowDef);\n\n\t\t\t\t\tn->name = NULL;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->refname = $2;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->partitionClause = $3;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->orderClause = $4;\n\t\t\t\t\t/* copy relevant fields of opt_frame_clause */\n\t\t\t\t\tn->frameOptions = $5->frameOptions;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->startOffset = $5->startOffset;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->endOffset = $5->endOffset;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->location = @1;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = n;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t;\n\n/*\n * If we see PARTITION, RANGE, ROWS or GROUPS as the first token after the '('\n * of a window_specification, we want the assumption to be that there is\n * no existing_window_name; but those keywords are unreserved and so could\n * be ColIds.  We fix this by making them have the same precedence as IDENT\n * and giving the empty production here a slightly higher precedence, so\n * that the shift/reduce conflict is resolved in favor of reducing the rule.\n * These keywords are thus precluded from being an existing_window_name but\n * are not reserved for any other purpose.\n */\nopt_existing_window_name: ColId\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = $1; }\n\t\t\t| /*EMPTY*/\t\t\t\t%prec Op\t\t{ $$ = NULL; }\n\t\t;\n\nopt_partition_clause: PARTITION BY expr_list\t\t{ $$ = $3; }\n\t\t\t| /*EMPTY*/\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = NIL; }\n\t\t;\n\n/*\n * For frame clauses, we return a WindowDef, but only some fields are used:\n * frameOptions, startOffset, and endOffset.\n */\nopt_frame_clause:\n\t\t\tRANGE frame_extent opt_window_exclusion_clause\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tWindowDef  *n = $2;\n\n\t\t\t\t\tn->frameOptions |= FRAMEOPTION_NONDEFAULT | FRAMEOPTION_RANGE;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->frameOptions |= $3;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = n;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| ROWS frame_extent opt_window_exclusion_clause\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tWindowDef  *n = $2;\n\n\t\t\t\t\tn->frameOptions |= FRAMEOPTION_NONDEFAULT | FRAMEOPTION_ROWS;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->frameOptions |= $3;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = n;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| GROUPS frame_extent opt_window_exclusion_clause\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tWindowDef  *n = = makeNode(WindowDef);\n\n\t\t\t\t\tn->frameOptions = FRAMEOPTION_DEFAULTS;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->startOffset = NULL;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->endOffset = NULL;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = n;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t;\n\nframe_extent: frame_bound\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tWindowDef  *n = $1;\n\n\t\t\t\t\t/* reject invalid cases */\n\t\t\t\t\tif (n->frameOptions & FRAMEOPTION_START_UNBOUNDED_FOLLOWING)\n\t\t\t\t\t\tereport(ERROR,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t(errcode(ERRCODE_WINDOWING_ERROR),\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t errmsg(\"frame start cannot be UNBOUNDED FOLLOWING\"),\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t parser_errposition(@1)));\n\t\t\t\t\tif (n->frameOptions & FRAMEOPTION_START_OFFSET_FOLLOWING)\n\t\t\t\t\t\tereport(ERROR,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t(errcode(ERRCODE_WINDOWING_ERROR),\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t errmsg(\"frame starting from following row cannot end with current row\"),\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t parser_errposition(@1)));\n\t\t\t\t\tn->frameOptions |= FRAMEOPTION_END_CURRENT_ROW;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = n;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| BETWEEN frame_bound AND frame_bound\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tWindowDef  *n1 = $2;\n\t\t\t\t\tWindowDef  *n2 = $4;\n\n\t\t\t\t\t/* form merged options */\n\t\t\t\t\tint\t\tframeOptions = n1->frameOptions;\n\t\t\t\t\t/* shift converts START_ options to END_ options */\n\t\t\t\t\tframeOptions |= n2->frameOptions << 1;\n\t\t\t\t\tframeOptions |= FRAMEOPTION_BETWEEN;\n\t\t\t\t\t/* reject invalid cases */\n\t\t\t\t\tif (frameOptions & FRAMEOPTION_START_UNBOUNDED_FOLLOWING)\n\t\t\t\t\t\tereport(ERROR,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t(errcode(ERRCODE_WINDOWING_ERROR),\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t errmsg(\"frame start cannot be UNBOUNDED FOLLOWING\"),\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t parser_errposition(@2)));\n\t\t\t\t\tif (frameOptions & FRAMEOPTION_END_UNBOUNDED_PRECEDING)\n\t\t\t\t\t\tereport(ERROR,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t(errcode(ERRCODE_WINDOWING_ERROR),\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t errmsg(\"frame end cannot be UNBOUNDED PRECEDING\"),\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t parser_errposition(@4)));\n\t\t\t\t\tif ((frameOptions & FRAMEOPTION_START_CURRENT_ROW) &&\n\t\t\t\t\t\t(frameOptions & FRAMEOPTION_END_OFFSET_PRECEDING))\n\t\t\t\t\t\tereport(ERROR,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t(errcode(ERRCODE_WINDOWING_ERROR),\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t errmsg(\"frame starting from current row cannot have preceding rows\"),\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t parser_errposition(@4)));\n\t\t\t\t\tif ((frameOptions & FRAMEOPTION_START_OFFSET_FOLLOWING) &&\n\t\t\t\t\t\t(frameOptions & (FRAMEOPTION_END_OFFSET_PRECEDING |\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t FRAMEOPTION_END_CURRENT_ROW)))\n\t\t\t\t\t\tereport(ERROR,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t(errcode(ERRCODE_WINDOWING_ERROR),\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t errmsg(\"frame starting from following row cannot have preceding rows\"),\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t parser_errposition(@4)));\n\t\t\t\t\tn1->frameOptions = frameOptions;\n\t\t\t\t\tn1->endOffset = n2->startOffset;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = n1;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t;\n\n/*\n * This is used for both frame start and frame end, with output set up on\n * the assumption it's frame start; the frame_extent productions must reject\n * invalid cases.\n */\nframe_bound:\n\t\t\tUNBOUNDED PRECEDING\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tWindowDef  *n = makeNode(WindowDef);\n\n\t\t\t\t\tn->frameOptions = FRAMEOPTION_START_UNBOUNDED_PRECEDING;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->startOffset = NULL;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->endOffset = NULL;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = n;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| UNBOUNDED FOLLOWING\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tWindowDef  *n = makeNode(WindowDef);\n\n\t\t\t\t\tn->frameOptions = FRAMEOPTION_START_UNBOUNDED_FOLLOWING;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->startOffset = NULL;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->endOffset = NULL;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = n;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| CURRENT_P ROW\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tWindowDef  *n = makeNode(WindowDef);\n\n\t\t\t\t\tn->frameOptions = FRAMEOPTION_START_CURRENT_ROW;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->startOffset = NULL;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->endOffset = FRAMEOPTION_START_OFFSET_PRECEDING;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->startOffset = $1;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->endOffset = NULL;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = n;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| a_expr FOLLOWING\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tWindowDef  *n = EXCLUDE TIES\t\t\t{ $$ = FRAMEOPTION_EXCLUDE_TIES; }\n\t\t\t| EXCLUDE NO OTHERS\t\t{ $$ = 0; }\n\t\t\t| /*EMPTY*/\t\t\t\t{ $$ = 0; }\n\t\t;\n\n\n/*\n * Supporting nonterminals for expressions.\n */\n\n/* Explicit row production.\n *\n * SQL99 allows an optional ROW keyword, so we can now do single-element rows\n * without conflicting with the parenthesized a_expr production.  Without the\n * ROW keyword, there must be more than one a_expr inside the parens.\n */\nrow:\t\tROW '(' expr_list ')'\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = $3; }\n\t\t\t| ROW '(' ')'\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = NIL; }\n\t\t\t| '(' expr_list ',' a_expr ')'\t\t\t{ $$ = lappend($2, $4); }\n\t\t;\n\nexplicit_row:\tROW '(' expr_list ')'\t\t\t\t{ $$ = $3; }\n\t\t\t| ROW '(' ')'\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = NIL; }\n\t\t;\n\nimplicit_row:\t'(' expr_list ',' a_expr ')'\t\t{ $$ = lappend($2, $4); }\n\t\t;\n\nsub_type:\tANY\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = ANY_SUBLINK; }\n\t\t\t| SOME\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = ANY_SUBLINK; }\n\t\t\t| ALL\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = ALL_SUBLINK; }\n\t\t;\n\nall_Op:\t\tOp\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = $1; }\n\t\t\t| MathOp\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = $1; }\n\t\t;\n\nMathOp:\t\t '+'\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = \"+\"; }\n\t\t\t| '-'\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = \"-\"; }\n\t\t\t| '*'\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = \"*\"; }\n\t\t\t| '/'\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = \"/\"; }\n\t\t\t| '%'\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = \"%\"; }\n\t\t\t| '^'\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = \"^\"; }\n\t\t\t| '<'\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = \"<\"; }\n\t\t\t| '>'\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = \">\"; }\n\t\t\t| '='\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = \"=\"; }\n\t\t\t| LESS_EQUALS\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = \"<=\"; }\n\t\t\t| GREATER_EQUALS\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = \">=\"; }\n\t\t\t| NOT_EQUALS\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = \"<>\"; }\n\t\t;\n\nqual_Op:\tOp\n\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = list_make1(makeString($1)); }\n\t\t\t| OPERATOR '(' any_operator ')'\n\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = $3; }\n\t\t;\n\nqual_all_Op:\n\t\t\tall_Op\n\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = list_make1(makeString($1)); }\n\t\t\t| OPERATOR '(' any_operator ')'\n\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = $3; }\n\t\t;\n\nsubquery_Op:\n\t\t\tall_Op\n\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = list_make1(makeString($1)); }\n\t\t\t| OPERATOR '(' any_operator ')'\n\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = $3; }\n\t\t\t| LIKE\n\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = list_make1(makeString(\"~~\")); }\n\t\t\t| NOT_LA LIKE\n\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = list_make1(makeString(\"!~~\")); }\n\t\t\t| ILIKE\n\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = list_make1(makeString(\"~~*\")); }\n\t\t\t| NOT_LA ILIKE\n\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = list_make1(makeString(\"!~~*\")); }\n/* cannot put SIMILAR TO here, because SIMILAR TO is a hack.\n * the regular expression is preprocessed by a function (similar_to_escape),\n * and the ~ operator for posix regular expressions is used.\n *        x SIMILAR TO y     ->    x ~ similar_to_escape(y)\n * this transformation is made on the fly by the parser upwards.\n * however the SubLink structure which handles any/some/all stuff\n * is not ready for such a thing.\n */\n\t\t\t;\n\nexpr_list:\ta_expr\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = list_make1($1);\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| expr_list ',' a_expr\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = lappend($1, $3);\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t;\n\n/* function arguments can have names */\nfunc_arg_list:  func_arg_expr\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = list_make1($1);\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| func_arg_list ',' func_arg_expr\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = lappend($1, $3);\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t;\n\nfunc_arg_expr:  a_expr\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = $1;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| param_name COLON_EQUALS a_expr\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tNamedArgExpr *na = makeNode(NamedArgExpr);\n\n\t\t\t\t\tna->name = $1;\n\t\t\t\t\tna->arg = (Expr *) $3;\n\t\t\t\t\tna->argnumber = -1;\t\t/* until determined */\n\t\t\t\t\tna->location = @1;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = (Node *) na;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| param_name EQUALS_GREATER a_expr\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tNamedArgExpr *na = makeNode(NamedArgExpr);\n\n\t\t\t\t\tna->name = $1;\n\t\t\t\t\tna->arg = (Expr *) $3;\n\t\t\t\t\tna->argnumber = -1;\t\t/* until determined */\n\t\t\t\t\tna->location = @1;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = (Node *) na;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t;\n\nfunc_arg_list_opt:\tfunc_arg_list\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = $1; }\n\t\t\t| /*EMPTY*/\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = NIL; }\n\t\t;\n\ntype_list:\tTypename\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = list_make1($1); }\n\t\t\t| type_list ',' Typename\t\t\t\t{ $$ = lappend($1, $3); }\n\t\t;\n\narray_expr: '[' expr_list ']'\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = makeAArrayExpr($2, @1, @3);\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| '[' array_expr_list ']'\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = makeAArrayExpr($2, @1, @3);\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| '[' ']'\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = makeAArrayExpr(NIL, @1, @2);\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t;\n\narray_expr_list: array_expr\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = list_make1($1); }\n\t\t\t| array_expr_list ',' array_expr\t\t{ $$ = lappend($1, $3); }\n\t\t;\n\n\nextract_list:\n\t\t\textract_arg FROM a_expr\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = list_make2(makeStringConst($1, @1), $3);\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t;\n\n/* Allow delimited string Sconst in extract_arg as an SQL extension.\n * - thomas 2001-04-12\n */\nextract_arg:\n\t\t\tIDENT\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = $1; }\n\t\t\t| YEAR_P\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = \"year\"; }\n\t\t\t| MONTH_P\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = \"month\"; }\n\t\t\t| DAY_P\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = \"day\"; }\n\t\t\t| HOUR_P\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = \"hour\"; }\n\t\t\t| MINUTE_P\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = \"minute\"; }\n\t\t\t| SECOND_P\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = \"second\"; }\n\t\t\t| Sconst\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = $1; }\n\t\t;\n\nunicode_normal_form:\n\t\t\tNFC\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = \"NFC\"; }\n\t\t\t| NFD\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = \"NFD\"; }\n\t\t\t| NFKC\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = \"NFKC\"; }\n\t\t\t| NFKD\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = \"NFKD\"; }\n\t\t;\n\n/* OVERLAY() arguments */\noverlay_list:\n\t\t\ta_expr PLACING a_expr FROM a_expr FOR a_expr\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t/* overlay(A PLACING B FROM C FOR D) is converted to overlay(A, B, C, D) */\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = list_make4($1, $3, $5, $7);\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| a_expr PLACING a_expr FROM a_expr\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t/* overlay(A PLACING B FROM C) is converted to overlay(A, B, C) */\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = list_make3($1, $3, $5);\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t;\n\n/* position_list uses b_expr not a_expr to avoid conflict with general IN */\nposition_list:\n\t\t\tb_expr IN_P b_expr\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = list_make2($3, $1); }\n\t\t;\n\n/*\n * SUBSTRING() arguments\n *\n * Note that SQL:1999 has both\n *     text FROM int FOR int\n * and\n *     text FROM pattern FOR escape\n *\n * In the parser we map them both to a call to the substring() function and\n * rely on type resolution to pick the right one.\n *\n * In SQL:2003, the second variant was changed to\n *     text SIMILAR pattern ESCAPE escape\n * We could in theory map that to a different function internally, but\n * since we still support the SQL:1999 version, we don't.  However,\n * ruleutils.c will reverse-list the call in the newer style.\n */\nsubstr_list:\n\t\t\ta_expr FROM a_expr FOR a_expr\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = list_make3($1, $3, $5);\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| a_expr FOR a_expr FROM a_expr\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t/* not legal per SQL, but might as well allow it */\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = list_make3($1, $5, $3);\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| a_expr FROM a_expr\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t/*\n\t\t\t\t\t * Because we aren't restricting data types here, this\n\t\t\t\t\t * syntax can end up resolving to textregexsubstr().\n\t\t\t\t\t * We've historically allowed that to happen, so continue\n\t\t\t\t\t * to accept it.  However, ruleutils.c will reverse-list\n\t\t\t\t\t * such a call in regular function call syntax.\n\t\t\t\t\t */\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = list_make2($1, $3);\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| a_expr FOR a_expr\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t/* not legal per SQL */\n\n\t\t\t\t\t/*\n\t\t\t\t\t * Since there are no cases where this syntax allows\n\t\t\t\t\t * a textual FOR value, we forcibly cast the argument\n\t\t\t\t\t * to int4.  The possible matches in pg_proc are\n\t\t\t\t\t * substring(text,int4) and substring(text,text),\n\t\t\t\t\t * and we don't want the parser to choose the latter,\n\t\t\t\t\t * which it is likely to do if the second argument\n\t\t\t\t\t * is unknown or doesn't have an implicit cast to int4.\n\t\t\t\t\t */\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = list_make3($1, makeIntConst(1, -1),\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tmakeTypeCast($3,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t SystemTypeName(\"int4\"), -1));\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| a_expr SIMILAR a_expr ESCAPE a_expr\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = list_make3($1, $3, $5);\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t;\n\ntrim_list:\ta_expr FROM expr_list\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = lappend($3, $1); }\n\t\t\t| FROM expr_list\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = $2; }\n\t\t\t| expr_list\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = $1; }\n\t\t;\n\n/*\n * Define SQL-style CASE clause.\n * - Full specification\n *\tCASE WHEN a = b THEN c ... ELSE d END\n * - Implicit argument\n *\tCASE a WHEN b THEN c ... ELSE d END\n */\ncase_expr:\tCASE case_arg when_clause_list case_default END_P\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tCaseExpr   *c = makeNode(CaseExpr);\n\n\t\t\t\t\tc->casetype = InvalidOid; /* not analyzed yet */\n\t\t\t\t\tc->arg = (Expr *) $2;\n\t\t\t\t\tc->args = $3;\n\t\t\t\t\tc->defresult = (Expr *) $4;\n\t\t\t\t\tc->location = @1;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = (Node *) c;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t;\n\nwhen_clause_list:\n\t\t\t/* There must be at least one */\n\t\t\twhen_clause\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = list_make1($1); }\n\t\t\t| when_clause_list when_clause\t\t\t{ $$ = lappend($1, $2); }\n\t\t;\n\nwhen_clause:\n\t\t\tWHEN a_expr THEN a_expr\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tCaseWhen   *w = makeNode(CaseWhen);\n\n\t\t\t\t\tw->expr = (Expr *) $2;\n\t\t\t\t\tw->result = (Expr *) $4;\n\t\t\t\t\tw->location = @1;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = (Node *) w;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t;\n\ncase_default:\n\t\t\tELSE a_expr\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = $2; }\n\t\t\t| /*EMPTY*/\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = NULL; }\n\t\t;\n\ncase_arg:\ta_expr\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = $1; }\n\t\t\t| /*EMPTY*/\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = NULL; }\n\t\t;\n\ncolumnref:\tColId\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = makeColumnRef($1, NIL, @1, yyscanner);\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| ColId indirection\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = makeColumnRef($1, $2, @1, yyscanner);\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t;\n\nindirection_el:\n\t\t\t'.' attr_name\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = (Node *) makeString($2);\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| '.' '*'\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = (Node *) makeNode(A_Star);\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| '[' a_expr ']'\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tA_Indices *ai = makeNode(A_Indices);\n\n\t\t\t\t\tai->is_slice = false;\n\t\t\t\t\tai->lidx = NULL;\n\t\t\t\t\tai->uidx = $2;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = (Node *) ai;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| '[' opt_slice_bound ':' opt_slice_bound ']'\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tA_Indices *ai = makeNode(A_Indices);\n\n\t\t\t\t\tai->is_slice = true;\n\t\t\t\t\tai->lidx = $2;\n\t\t\t\t\tai->uidx = $4;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = (Node *) ai;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t;\n\nopt_slice_bound:\n\t\t\ta_expr\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = $1; }\n\t\t\t| opt_indirection indirection_el\t\t{ $$ = lappend($1, $2); }\n\t\t;\n\nopt_asymmetric: ASYMMETRIC\n\t\t\t| /*EMPTY*/\n\t\t;\n\n/* SQL/JSON support */\njson_passing_clause_opt:\n\t\t\tPASSING json_arguments\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = $2; }\n\t\t;\n\njson_argument:\n\t\t\tjson_value_expr AS ColLabel\n\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\tJsonArgument *n = makeNode(JsonArgument);\n\n\t\t\t\tn->val = (JsonValueExpr *) $1;\n\t\t\t\tn->name = $3;\n\t\t\t\t$$ = (Node *) n;\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t;\n\n/* ARRAY is a noise word */\njson_wrapper_behavior:\n\t\t\t  WITHOUT WRAPPER\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = JSW_NONE; }\n\t\t\t| WITHOUT ARRAY\tWRAPPER\t\t\t\t{ $$ = JSW_NONE; }\n\t\t\t| WITH WRAPPER\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = JSW_UNCONDITIONAL; }\n\t\t\t| WITH ARRAY WRAPPER\t\t\t\t{ $$ = JSW_UNCONDITIONAL; }\n\t\t\t| WITH CONDITIONAL ARRAY WRAPPER\t{ $$ = JSW_CONDITIONAL; }\n\t\t\t| WITH UNCONDITIONAL ARRAY WRAPPER\t{ $$ = JSW_UNCONDITIONAL; }\n\t\t\t| WITH CONDITIONAL WRAPPER\t\t\t{ $$ = JSW_CONDITIONAL; }\n\t\t\t| WITH UNCONDITIONAL WRAPPER\t\t{ $$ = JSW_UNCONDITIONAL; }\n\t\t\t| /* empty */\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = JSW_UNSPEC; }\n\t\t;\n\njson_behavior:\n\t\t\tDEFAULT a_expr\n\t\t\t\t{ $$ = (Node *) makeJsonBehavior(JSON_BEHAVIOR_DEFAULT, $2, @1); }\n\t\t\t| json_behavior_type\n\t\t\t\t{ $$ = (Node *) makeJsonBehavior($1, NULL, @1); }\n\t\t\t| KEEP QUOTES\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = JS_QUOTES_KEEP; }\n\t\t\t| OMIT QUOTES ON SCALAR STRING_P\t{ $$ = JS_QUOTES_OMIT; }\n\t\t\t| OMIT QUOTES\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = JS_QUOTES_OMIT; }\n\t\t\t| /* EMPTY */\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = JS_QUOTES_UNSPEC; }\n\t\t;\n\njson_returning_clause_opt:\n\t\t\tRETURNING Typename json_format_clause_opt\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tJsonOutput *n = makeNode(JsonOutput);\n\n\t\t\t\t\tn->typeName = $2;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->returning = makeNode(JsonReturning);\n\t\t\t\t\tn->returning->format = (JsonFormat *) $3;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = (Node *) n;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| /* EMPTY */\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = NULL; }\n\t\t;\n\n/*\n * We must assign the only-JSON production a precedence less than IDENT in\n * order to favor shifting over reduction when JSON is followed by VALUE_P,\n * OBJECT_P, or SCALAR.  (ARRAY doesn't need that treatment, because it's a\n * fully reserved word.)  Because json_predicate_type_constraint is always\n * followed by json_key_uniqueness_constraint_opt, we also need the only-JSON\n * production to have precedence less than WITH and WITHOUT.  UNBOUNDED isn't\n * really related to this syntax, but it's a convenient choice because it\n * already has a precedence less than IDENT for other reasons.\n */\njson_predicate_type_constraint:\n\t\t\tJSON\t\t\t\t\t%prec UNBOUNDED\t{ $$ = JS_TYPE_ANY; }\n\t\t\t| JSON VALUE_P\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = JS_TYPE_ANY; }\n\t\t\t| JSON ARRAY\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = JS_TYPE_ARRAY; }\n\t\t\t| JSON OBJECT_P\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = JS_TYPE_OBJECT; }\n\t\t\t| JSON SCALAR\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = JS_TYPE_SCALAR; }\n\t\t;\n\n/*\n * KEYS is a noise word here.  To avoid shift/reduce conflicts, assign the\n * KEYS-less productions a precedence less than IDENT (i.e., less than KEYS).\n * This prevents reducing them when the next token is KEYS.\n */\njson_key_uniqueness_constraint_opt:\n\t\t\tWITH UNIQUE KEYS\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = true; }\n\t\t\t| WITH UNIQUE\t\t\t\t%prec UNBOUNDED\t{ $$ = true; }\n\t\t\t| WITHOUT UNIQUE KEYS\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = false; }\n\t\t\t| WITHOUT UNIQUE\t\t\t%prec UNBOUNDED\t{ $$ = false; }\n\t\t\t| /* EMPTY */ \t\t\t\t%prec UNBOUNDED\t{ $$ = false; }\n\t\t;\n\njson_name_and_value_list:\n\t\t\tjson_name_and_value\n\t\t\t\t{ $$ = list_make1($1); }\n\t\t\t| json_name_and_value_list ',' json_name_and_value\n\t\t\t\t{ $$ = lappend($1, $3); }\n\t\t;\n\njson_name_and_value:\n/* Supporting this syntax seems to require major surgery\n\t\t\tKEY c_expr VALUE_P json_value_expr\n\t\t\t\t{ $$ = makeJsonKeyValue($2, $4); }\n\t\t\t| /* EMPTY */\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = NIL; }\n\t\t;\n\n/*****************************************************************************\n *\n *\ttarget list for SELECT\n *\n *****************************************************************************/\n\nopt_target_list: target_list\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = $1; }\n\t\t\t| /* EMPTY */\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = NIL; }\n\t\t;\n\ntarget_list:\n\t\t\ttarget_el\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = list_make1($1); }\n\t\t\t| target_list ',' target_el\t\t\t\t{ $$ = lappend($1, $3); }\n\t\t;\n\ntarget_el:\ta_expr AS ColLabel\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = makeNode(ResTarget);\n\t\t\t\t\t$$->name = $3;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$->indirection = NIL;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$->val = (Node *) $1;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$->location = @1;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| a_expr BareColLabel\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = makeNode(ResTarget);\n\t\t\t\t\t$$->name = $2;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$->indirection = NIL;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$->val = (Node *) $1;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$->location = @1;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| a_expr\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = makeNode(ResTarget);\n\t\t\t\t\t$$->name = NULL;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$->indirection = NIL;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$->val = (Node *) $1;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$->location = @1;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| '*'\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tColumnRef  *n = makeNode(ColumnRef);\n\n\t\t\t\t\tn->fields = list_make1(makeNode(A_Star));\n\t\t\t\t\tn->location = @1;\n\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = makeNode(ResTarget);\n\t\t\t\t\t$$->name = NULL;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$->indirection = NIL;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$->val = (Node *) n;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$->location = @1;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t;\n\n\n/*****************************************************************************\n *\n *\tNames and constants\n *\n *****************************************************************************/\n\nqualified_name_list:\n\t\t\tqualified_name\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = list_make1($1); }\n\t\t\t| qualified_name_list ',' qualified_name { $$ = lappend($1, $3); }\n\t\t;\n\n/*\n * The production for a qualified relation name has to exactly match the\n * production for a qualified func_name, because in a FROM clause we cannot\n * tell which we are parsing until we see what comes after it ('(' for a\n * func_name, something else for a relation). Therefore we allow 'indirection'\n * which may contain subscripts, and reject that case in the C code.\n */\nqualified_name:\n\t\t\tColId\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = makeRangeVar(NULL, $1, @1);\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| ColId indirection\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = makeRangeVarFromQualifiedName($1, $2, @1, yyscanner);\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t;\n\nname_list:\tname\n\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = list_make1(makeString($1)); }\n\t\t\t| name_list ',' name\n\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = lappend($1, makeString($3)); }\n\t\t;\n\n\nname:\t\tColId\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = $1; };\n\nattr_name:\tColLabel\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = $1; };\n\nfile_name:\tSconst\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = $1; };\n\n/*\n * The production for a qualified func_name has to exactly match the\n * production for a qualified columnref, because we cannot tell which we\n * are parsing until we see what comes after it ('(' or Sconst for a func_name,\n * anything else for a columnref).  Therefore we allow 'indirection' which\n * may contain subscripts, and reject that case in the C code.  (If we\n * ever implement SQL99-like methods, such syntax may actually become legal!)\n */\nfunc_name:\ttype_function_name\n\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = list_make1(makeString($1)); }\n\t\t\t| ColId indirection\n\t\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t\t$$ = check_func_name(lcons(makeString($1), $2),\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t yyscanner);\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t;\n\n\n/*\n * Constants\n */\nAexprConst: Iconst\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = makeIntConst($1, @1);\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| FCONST\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = makeFloatConst($1, @1);\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| Sconst\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = makeStringConst($1, @1);\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| BCONST\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = makeBitStringConst($1, @1);\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| XCONST\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t/* This is a bit constant per SQL99:\n\t\t\t\t\t * Without Feature F511, \"BIT data type\",\n\t\t\t\t\t * a <general literal> shall not be a\n\t\t\t\t\t * <bit string literal> or a <hex string literal>.\n\t\t\t\t\t */\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = makeBitStringConst($1, @1);\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| func_name Sconst\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t/* generic type 'literal' syntax */\n\t\t\t\t\tTypeName   *t = makeTypeNameFromNameList($1);\n\n\t\t\t\t\tt->location = @1;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = makeStringConstCast($2, @2, t);\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| func_name '(' func_arg_list opt_sort_clause ')' Sconst\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t/* generic syntax with a type modifier */\n\t\t\t\t\tTypeName   *t = makeTypeNameFromNameList($1);\n\t\t\t\t\tListCell   *lc;\n\n\t\t\t\t\t/*\n\t\t\t\t\t * We must use func_arg_list and opt_sort_clause in the\n\t\t\t\t\t * production to avoid reduce/reduce conflicts, but we\n\t\t\t\t\t * don't actually wish to allow NamedArgExpr in this\n\t\t\t\t\t * context, nor ORDER BY.\n\t\t\t\t\t */\n\t\t\t\t\tforeach(lc, $3)\n\t\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t\tNamedArgExpr *arg = (NamedArgExpr *) lfirst(lc);\n\n\t\t\t\t\t\tif (IsA(arg, NamedArgExpr))\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\tereport(ERROR,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t(errcode(ERRCODE_SYNTAX_ERROR),\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t errmsg(\"type modifier cannot have parameter name\"),\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t parser_errposition(arg->location)));\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t\tif ($4 != NIL)\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\tereport(ERROR,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t(errcode(ERRCODE_SYNTAX_ERROR),\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t errmsg(\"type modifier cannot have ORDER BY\"),\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t parser_errposition(@4)));\n\n\t\t\t\t\tt->typmods = $3;\n\t\t\t\t\tt->location = @1;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = makeStringConstCast($6, @6, t);\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| ConstTypename Sconst\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = makeStringConstCast($2, @2, $1);\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| ConstInterval Sconst opt_interval\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tTypeName   *t = $1;\n\n\t\t\t\t\tt->typmods = $3;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = makeStringConstCast($2, @2, t);\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| ConstInterval '(' Iconst ')' Sconst\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tTypeName   *t = $1;\n\n\t\t\t\t\tt->typmods = list_make2(makeIntConst(INTERVAL_FULL_RANGE, -1),\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tmakeIntConst($3, @3));\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = makeStringConstCast($5, @5, t);\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| TRUE_P\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = makeBoolAConst(true, @1);\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| FALSE_P\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = makeBoolAConst(false, @1);\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| NULL_P\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = makeNullAConst(@1);\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t;\n\nIconst:\t\tICONST\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = $1; };\nSconst:\t\tSCONST\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = $1; };\n\nSignedIconst: Iconst\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = $1; }\n\t\t\t| '+' Iconst\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = + $2; }\n\t\t\t| '-' Iconst\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = - $2; }\n\t\t;\n\n/* Role specifications */\nRoleId:\t\tRoleSpec\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tRoleSpec   *spc = (RoleSpec *) $1;\n\n\t\t\t\t\tswitch (spc->roletype)\n\t\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t\tcase ROLESPEC_CSTRING:\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t$$ = spc->rolename;\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\tbreak;\n\t\t\t\t\t\tcase ROLESPEC_PUBLIC:\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\tereport(ERROR,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t(errcode(ERRCODE_RESERVED_NAME),\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t errmsg(\"role name \\\"%s\\\" is reserved\",\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\"public\"),\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t parser_errposition(@1)));\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\tbreak;\n\t\t\t\t\t\tcase ROLESPEC_SESSION_USER:\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\tereport(ERROR,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t(errcode(ERRCODE_RESERVED_NAME),\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t errmsg(\"%s cannot be used as a role name here\",\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\"SESSION_USER\"),\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t parser_errposition(@1)));\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\tbreak;\n\t\t\t\t\t\tcase ROLESPEC_CURRENT_USER:\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\tereport(ERROR,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t(errcode(ERRCODE_RESERVED_NAME),\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t errmsg(\"%s cannot be used as a role name here\",\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\"CURRENT_USER\"),\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t parser_errposition(@1)));\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\tbreak;\n\t\t\t\t\t\tcase ROLESPEC_CURRENT_ROLE:\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\tereport(ERROR,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t(errcode(ERRCODE_RESERVED_NAME),\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t errmsg(\"%s cannot be used as a role name here\",\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\"CURRENT_ROLE\"),\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t parser_errposition(@1)));\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\tbreak;\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t;\n\nRoleSpec:\tNonReservedWord\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t/*\n\t\t\t\t\t * \"public\" and \"none\" are not keywords, but they must\n\t\t\t\t\t * be treated specially here.\n\t\t\t\t\t */\n\t\t\t\t\tRoleSpec   *n;\n\n\t\t\t\t\tif (strcmp($1, \"public\") == 0)\n\t\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t\tn = (RoleSpec *) makeRoleSpec(ROLESPEC_PUBLIC, @1);\n\t\t\t\t\t\tn->roletype = ROLESPEC_PUBLIC;\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t\telse if (strcmp($1, \"none\") == 0)\n\t\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t\tereport(ERROR,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t(errcode(ERRCODE_RESERVED_NAME),\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t errmsg(\"role name \\\"%s\\\" is reserved\",\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\"none\"),\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t parser_errposition(@1)));\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t\telse\n\t\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t\tn = makeRoleSpec(ROLESPEC_CSTRING, @1);\n\t\t\t\t\t\tn->rolename = pstrdup($1);\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = n;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| CURRENT_ROLE\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = makeRoleSpec(ROLESPEC_CURRENT_ROLE, @1);\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| CURRENT_USER\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = makeRoleSpec(ROLESPEC_CURRENT_USER, @1);\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| SESSION_USER\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = makeRoleSpec(ROLESPEC_SESSION_USER, @1);\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t;\n\nrole_list:\tRoleSpec\n\t\t\t\t{ $$ = list_make1($1); }\n\t\t\t| role_list ',' RoleSpec\n\t\t\t\t{ $$ = lappend($1, $3); }\n\t\t;\n\n\n/*****************************************************************************\n *\n * PL/pgSQL extensions\n *\n * You'd think a PL/pgSQL \"expression\" should be just an a_expr, but\n * historically it can include just about anything that can follow SELECT.\n * Therefore the returned struct is a SelectStmt.\n *****************************************************************************/\n\nPLpgSQL_Expr: opt_distinct_clause opt_target_list\n\t\t\tfrom_clause where_clause\n\t\t\tgroup_clause having_clause window_clause\n\t\t\topt_sort_clause opt_select_limit opt_for_locking_clause\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tSelectStmt *n = makeNode(SelectStmt);\n\n\t\t\t\t\tn->distinctClause = $1;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->targetList = $2;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->fromClause = $3;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->whereClause = $4;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->groupClause = ($5)->list;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->groupDistinct = ($5)->distinct;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->havingClause = $6;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->windowClause = $7;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->sortClause = $8;\n\t\t\t\t\tif ($9)\n\t\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t\tn->limitOffset = $9->limitOffset;\n\t\t\t\t\t\tn->limitCount = $9->limitCount;\n\t\t\t\t\t\tif (!n->sortClause &&\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t$9->limitOption == LIMIT_OPTION_WITH_TIES)\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\tereport(ERROR,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t(errcode(ERRCODE_SYNTAX_ERROR),\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t errmsg(\"WITH TIES cannot be specified without ORDER BY clause\"),\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t parser_errposition($9->optionLoc)));\n\t\t\t\t\t\tn->limitOption = $9->limitOption;\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t\tn->lockingClause = $10;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = (Node *) n;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t;\n\n/*\n * PL/pgSQL Assignment statement: name opt_indirection := PLpgSQL_Expr\n */\n\nPLAssignStmt: plassign_target opt_indirection plassign_equals PLpgSQL_Expr\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tPLAssignStmt *n = makeNode(PLAssignStmt);\n\n\t\t\t\t\tn->name = $1;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->indirection = check_indirection($2, yyscanner);\n\t\t\t\t\t/* nnames will be filled by calling production */\n\t\t\t\t\tn->val = (SelectStmt *) $4;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->location = @1;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = (Node *) n;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t;\n\nplassign_target: ColId\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = $1; }\n\t\t\t| PARAM\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = psprintf(\"$%d\", $1); }\n\t\t;\n\nplassign_equals: COLON_EQUALS\n\t\t\t| '='\n\t\t;\n\n\n/*\n * Name classification hierarchy.\n *\n * IDENT is the lexeme returned by the lexer for identifiers that match\n * no known keyword.  In most cases, we can accept certain keywords as\n * names, not only IDENTs.\tWe prefer to accept as many such keywords\n * as possible to minimize the impact of \"reserved words\" on programmers.\n * So, we divide names into several possible classes.  The classification\n * is chosen in part to make keywords acceptable as names wherever possible.\n */\n\n/* Column identifier --- names that can be column, table, etc names.\n */\nColId:\t\tIDENT\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = $1; }\n\t\t\t| unreserved_keyword\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = pstrdup($1); }\n\t\t\t| col_name_keyword\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = pstrdup($1); }\n\t\t;\n\n/* Type/function identifier --- names that can be type or function names.\n */\ntype_function_name:\tIDENT\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = $1; }\n\t\t\t| unreserved_keyword\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = pstrdup($1); }\n\t\t\t| type_func_name_keyword\t\t\t\t{ $$ = pstrdup($1); }\n\t\t;\n\n/* Any not-fully-reserved word --- these names can be, eg, role names.\n */\nNonReservedWord:\tIDENT\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = $1; }\n\t\t\t| unreserved_keyword\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = pstrdup($1); }\n\t\t\t| col_name_keyword\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = pstrdup($1); }\n\t\t\t| type_func_name_keyword\t\t\t\t{ $$ = pstrdup($1); }\n\t\t;\n\n/* Column label --- allowed labels in \"AS\" clauses.\n * This presently includes *all* Postgres keywords.\n */\nColLabel:\tIDENT\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = $1; }\n\t\t\t| unreserved_keyword\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = pstrdup($1); }\n\t\t\t| col_name_keyword\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = pstrdup($1); }\n\t\t\t| type_func_name_keyword\t\t\t\t{ $$ = pstrdup($1); }\n\t\t\t| reserved_keyword\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = pstrdup($1); }\n\t\t;\n\n/* Bare column label --- names that can be column labels without writing \"AS\".\n * This classification is orthogonal to the other keyword categories.\n */\nBareColLabel:\tIDENT\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = $1; }\n\t\t\t| bare_label_keyword\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = pstrdup($1); }\n\t\t;\n\n\n/*\n * Keyword category lists.  Generally, every keyword present in\n * the Postgres grammar should appear in exactly one of these lists.\n *\n * Put a new keyword into the first list that it can go into without causing\n * shift or reduce conflicts.  The earlier lists define \"less reserved\"\n * categories of keywords.\n *\n * Make sure that each keyword's category in kwlist.h matches where\n * it is listed here.  (Someday we may be able to generate these lists and\n * kwlist.h's table from one source of truth.)\n */\n\n/* \"Unreserved\" keywords --- available for use as any kind of name.\n */\nunreserved_keyword:\n\t\t\t  ABORT_P\n\t\t\t| ABSENT\n\t\t\t| ABSOLUTE_P\n\t\t\t| ACCESS\n\t\t\t| ACTION\n\t\t\t| ADD_P\n\t\t\t| ADMIN\n\t\t\t| AFTER\n\t\t\t| AGGREGATE\n\t\t\t| ALSO\n\t\t\t| ALTER\n\t\t\t| ALWAYS\n\t\t\t| ASENSITIVE\n\t\t\t| ASSERTION\n\t\t\t| ASSIGNMENT\n\t\t\t| AT\n\t\t\t| ATOMIC\n\t\t\t| ATTACH\n\t\t\t| ATTRIBUTE\n\t\t\t| BACKWARD\n\t\t\t| BEFORE\n\t\t\t| BEGIN_P\n\t\t\t| BREADTH\n\t\t\t| BY\n\t\t\t| CACHE\n\t\t\t| CALL\n\t\t\t| CALLED\n\t\t\t| CASCADE\n\t\t\t| CASCADED\n\t\t\t| CATALOG_P\n\t\t\t| CHAIN\n\t\t\t| CHARACTERISTICS\n\t\t\t| CHECKPOINT\n\t\t\t| CLASS\n\t\t\t| CLOSE\n\t\t\t| CLUSTER\n\t\t\t| COLUMNS\n\t\t\t| COMMENT\n\t\t\t| COMMENTS\n\t\t\t| COMMIT\n\t\t\t| COMMITTED\n\t\t\t| COMPRESSION\n\t\t\t| CONDITIONAL\n\t\t\t| CONFIGURATION\n\t\t\t| CONFLICT\n\t\t\t| CONNECTION\n\t\t\t| CONSTRAINTS\n\t\t\t| CONTENT_P\n\t\t\t| CONTINUE_P\n\t\t\t| CONVERSION_P\n\t\t\t| COPY\n\t\t\t| COST\n\t\t\t| CSV\n\t\t\t| CUBE\n\t\t\t| YES_P\n\t\t\t| ZONE\n\t\t;\n\n/* Column identifier --- keywords that can be column, table, etc names.\n *\n * Many of these keywords will in fact be recognized as type or function\n * names too; but they have special productions for the purpose, and so\n * can't be treated as \"generic\" type or function names.\n *\n * The type names appearing here are not usable as function names\n * because they can be followed by '(' in typename productions, which\n * looks too much like a function call for an LR(1) parser.\n */\ncol_name_keyword:\n\t\t\t  BETWEEN\n\t\t\t| BIGINT\n\t\t\t| BIT\n\t\t\t| Most of these are keywords that are used as operators in expressions;\n * in general such keywords can't be column names because they would be\n * ambiguous with variables, but they are unambiguous as function identifiers.\n *\n * Do not include POSITION, SUBSTRING, etc here since they have explicit\n * productions in a_expr to support the goofy SQL9x argument syntax.\n * - thomas 2000-11-28\n */\ntype_func_name_keyword:\n\t\t\t  AUTHORIZATION\n\t\t\t| BINARY\n\t\t\t| COLLATION\n\t\t\t| CONCURRENTLY\n\t\t\t| CROSS\n\t\t\t| CURRENT_SCHEMA\n\t\t\t| FREEZE\n\t\t\t| FULL\n\t\t\t| ILIKE\n\t\t\t| SUBSTRING\n\t\t\t| SUPPORT\n\t\t\t| SYMMETRIC\n\t\t\t| SYSID\n\t\t\t| SYSTEM_P\n\t\t\t| SYSTEM_USER\n\t\t\t| TABLE\n\t\t\t| TABLES\n\t\t\t| TABLESAMPLE\n\t\t\t| TABLESPACE\n\t\t\t| TARGET\n\t\t\t| TEMP\n\t\t\t| TEMPLATE\n\t\t\t| TEMPORARY\n\t\t\t| TEXT_P\n\t\t\t| THEN\n\t\t\t| TIES\n\t\t\t| TIME\n\t\t\t| TIMESTAMP\n\t\t\t| TRAILING\n\t\t\t| TRANSACTION\n\t\t\t| TRANSFORM\n\t\t\t| TREAT\n\t\t\t| TRIGGER\n\t\t\t| TRIM\n\t\t\t| TRUE_P\n\t\t\t| TRUNCATE\n\t\t\t| TRUSTED\n\t\t\t| TYPE_P\n\t\t\t| TYPES_P\n\t\t\t| UESCAPE\n\t\t\t| UNBOUNDED\n\t\t\t| UNCOMMITTED\n\t\t\t| UNCONDITIONAL\n\t\t\t| UNENCRYPTED\n\t\t\t| UNIQUE\n\t\t\t| UNKNOWN\n\t\t\t| UNLISTEN\n\t\t\t| UNLOGGED\n\t\t\t| UNTIL\n\t\t\t| UPDATE\n\t\t\t| USER\n\t\t\t| USING\n\t\t\t| VACUUM\n\t\t\t| VALID\n\t\t\t| VALIDATE\n\t\t\t| VALIDATOR\n\t\t\t| VALUE_P\n\t\t\t| VALUES\n\t\t\t| VARCHAR\n\t\t\t| VARIADIC\n\t\t\t| VERBOSE\n\t\t\t| VERSION_P\n\t\t\t| VIEW\n\t\t\t| VIEWS\n\t\t\t| VIRTUAL\n\t\t\t| VOLATILE\n\t\t\t| WHEN\n\t\t\t| WHITESPACE_P\n\t\t\t| WORK\n\t\t\t| WRAPPER\n\t\t\t| WRITE\n\t\t\t| XML_P\n\t\t\t| XMLATTRIBUTES\n\t\t\t| XMLCONCAT\n\t\t\t| XMLELEMENT\n\t\t\t| XMLEXISTS\n\t\t\t| XMLFOREST\n\t\t\t| XMLNAMESPACES\n\t\t\t| XMLPARSE\n\t\t\t| XMLPI\n\t\t\t| XMLROOT\n\t\t\t| XMLSERIALIZE\n\t\t\t| XMLTABLE\n\t\t\t| YES_P\n\t\t\t| ZONE\n\t\t;\n\n%%\n\n/*\n * The signature of this function is required by bison.  However, we\n * ignore the passed yylloc and instead use the last token position\n * available from the scanner.\n */\nstatic void\nbase_yyerror(YYLTYPE *yylloc, core_yyscan_t yyscanner, const char *msg)\n{\n\tparser_yyerror(msg);\n}\n\nstatic RawStmt *\nmakeRawStmt(Node *stmt, int stmt_location)\n{\n\tRawStmt    *rs = makeNode(RawStmt);\n\n\trs->stmt = stmt;\n\trs->stmt_location = stmt_location;\n\trs->stmt_len = 0;\t\t\t/* might get changed later */\n\treturn rs;\n}\n\n/* Adjust a RawStmt to reflect that it doesn't run to the end of the string */\nstatic void\nupdateRawStmtEnd(RawStmt *rs, int end_location)\n{\n\t/*\n\t * If we already set the length, don't change it.  This is for situations\n\t * like \"select foo ;; select bar\" where the same statement will be last\n\t * in the string for more than one semicolon.\n\t */\n\tif (rs->stmt_len > 0)\n\t\treturn;\n\n\t/* OK, update length of RawStmt */\n\trs->stmt_len = end_location - rs->stmt_location;\n}\n\nstatic Node *\nmakeColumnRef(char *colname, List *indirection,\n\t\t\t  int location, core_yyscan_t yyscanner)\n{\n\t/*\n\t * Generate a ColumnRef node, with an A_Indirection node added if there is\n\t * any subscripting in the specified indirection list.  However, any field\n\t * selection at the start of the indirection list must be transposed into\n\t * the \"fields\" part of the ColumnRef node.\n\t */\n\tColumnRef  *c = makeNode(ColumnRef);\n\tint\t\t\tnfields = 0;\n\tListCell   *l;\n\n\tc->location = location;\n\tforeach(l, indirection)\n\t{\n\t\tif (IsA(lfirst(l), A_Indices))\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tA_Indirection *i = makeNode(A_Indirection);\n\n\t\t\tif (nfields == 0)\n\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t/* easy case - all indirection goes to A_Indirection */\n\t\t\t\tc->fields = list_make1(makeString(colname));\n\t\t\t\ti->indirection = check_indirection(indirection, yyscanner);\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\telse\n\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t/* got to split the list in two */\n\t\t\t\ti->indirection = check_indirection(list_copy_tail(indirection,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t  nfields),\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t   yyscanner);\n\t\t\t\tindirection = list_truncate(indirection, nfields);\n\t\t\t\tc->fields = lcons(makeString(colname), indirection);\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\ti->arg = (Node *) c;\n\t\t\treturn (Node *) i;\n\t\t}\n\t\telse if (IsA(lfirst(l), A_Star))\n\t\t{\n\t\t\t/* We only allow '*' at the end of a ColumnRef */\n\t\t\tif (lnext(indirection, l) != NULL)\n\t\t\t\tparser_yyerror(\"improper use of \\\"*\\\"\");\n\t\t}\n\t\tnfields++;\n\t}\n\t/* No subscripting, so all indirection gets added to field list */\n\tc->fields = lcons(makeString(colname), indirection);\n\treturn (Node *) c;\n}\n\nstatic Node *\nmakeTypeCast(Node *arg, TypeName *typename, int location)\n{\n\tTypeCast   *n = makeNode(TypeCast);\n\n\tn->arg = arg;\n\tn->typeName = typename;\n\tn->location = location;\n\treturn (Node *) n;\n}\n\nstatic Node *\nmakeStringConstCast(char *str, int location, TypeName *typename)\n{\n\tNode\t   *s = makeStringConst(str, location);\n\n\treturn makeTypeCast(s, typename, -1);\n}\n\nstatic Node *\nmakeIntConst(int val, int location)\n{\n\tA_Const    *n = makeNode(A_Const);\n\n\tn->val.ival.type = T_Integer;\n\tn->val.ival.ival = val;\n\tn->location = location;\n\n\treturn (Node *) n;\n}\n\nstatic Node *\nmakeFloatConst(char *str, int location)\n{\n\tA_Const    *n = makeNode(A_Const);\n\n\tn->val.fval.type = T_Float;\n\tn->val.fval.fval = str;\n\tn->location = location;\n\n\treturn (Node *) n;\n}\n\nstatic Node *\nmakeBoolAConst(bool state, int location)\n{\n\tA_Const    *n = makeNode(A_Const);\n\n\tn->val.boolval.type = T_Boolean;\n\tn->val.boolval.boolval = state;\n\tn->location = location;\n\n\treturn (Node *) n;\n}\n\nstatic Node *\nmakeBitStringConst(char *str, int location)\n{\n\tA_Const    *n = makeNode(A_Const);\n\n\tn->val.bsval.type = T_BitString;\n\tn->val.bsval.bsval = str;\n\tn->location = location;\n\n\treturn (Node *) n;\n}\n\nstatic Node *\nmakeNullAConst(int location)\n{\n\tA_Const    *n = makeNode(A_Const);\n\n\tn->isnull = true;\n\tn->location = location;\n\n\treturn (Node *) n;\n}\n\nstatic Node *\nmakeAConst(Node *v, int location)\n{\n\tNode\t   *n;\n\n\tswitch (v->type)\n\t{\n\t\tcase T_Float:\n\t\t\tn = makeFloatConst(castNode(Float, v)->fval, location);\n\t\t\tbreak;\n\n\t\tcase T_Integer:\n\t\t\tn = makeIntConst(castNode(Integer, v)->ival, location);\n\t\t\tbreak;\n\n\t\tdefault:\n\t\t\t/* currently not used */\n\t\t\tAssert(false);\n\t\t\tn = NULL;\n\t}\n\n\treturn n;\n}\n\n/* makeRoleSpec\n * Create a RoleSpec with the given type\n */\nstatic RoleSpec *\nmakeRoleSpec(RoleSpecType type, int location)\n{\n\tRoleSpec   *spec = makeNode(RoleSpec);\n\n\tspec->roletype = type;\n\tspec->location = location;\n\n\treturn spec;\n}\n\n/* check_qualified_name --- check the result of qualified_name production\n *\n * It's easiest to let the grammar production for qualified_name allow\n * subscripts and '*', which we then must reject here.\n */\nstatic void\ncheck_qualified_name(List *names, core_yyscan_t yyscanner)\n{\n\tListCell   *i;\n\n\tforeach(i, names)\n\t{\n\t\tif (!IsA(lfirst(i), String))\n\t\t\tparser_yyerror(\"syntax error\");\n\t}\n}\n\n/* check_func_name --- check the result of func_name production\n *\n * It's easiest to let the grammar production for func_name allow subscripts\n * and '*', which we then must reject here.\n */\nstatic List *\ncheck_func_name(List *names, core_yyscan_t yyscanner)\n{\n\tListCell   *i;\n\n\tforeach(i, names)\n\t{\n\t\tif (!IsA(lfirst(i), String))\n\t\t\tparser_yyerror(\"syntax error\");\n\t}\n\treturn names;\n}\n\n/* check_indirection --- check the result of indirection production\n *\n * We only allow '*' at the end of the list, but it's hard to enforce that\n * in the grammar, so do it here.\n */\nstatic List *\ncheck_indirection(List *indirection, core_yyscan_t yyscanner)\n{\n\tListCell   *l;\n\n\tforeach(l, indirection)\n\t{\n\t\tif (IsA(lfirst(l), A_Star))\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tif (lnext(indirection, l) != NULL)\n\t\t\t\tparser_yyerror(\"improper use of \\\"*\\\"\");\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn indirection;\n}\n\n/* extractArgTypes()\n * Given a list of FunctionParameter nodes, extract a list of just the\n * argument types (TypeNames) for input parameters only.  This is what\n * is needed to look up an existing function, which is what is wanted by\n * the productions that use this call.\n */\nstatic List *\nextractArgTypes(List *parameters)\n{\n\tList\t   *result = NIL;\n\tListCell   *i;\n\n\tforeach(i, parameters)\n\t{\n\t\tFunctionParameter *p = (FunctionParameter *) lfirst(i);\n\n\t\tif (p->mode != FUNC_PARAM_OUT && p->mode != FUNC_PARAM_TABLE)\n\t\t\tresult = lappend(result, p->argType);\n\t}\n\treturn result;\n}\n\n/* extractAggrArgTypes()\n * As above, but work from the output of the aggr_args production.\n */\nstatic List *\nextractAggrArgTypes(List *aggrargs)\n{\n\tAssert(list_length(aggrargs) == 2);\n\treturn extractArgTypes((List *) linitial(aggrargs));\n}\n\n/* makeOrderedSetArgs()\n * Build the result of the aggr_args production (which see the comments for).\n * This handles only the case where both given lists are nonempty, so that\n * we have to deal with multiple VARIADIC arguments.\n */\nstatic List *\nmakeOrderedSetArgs(List *directargs, List *orderedargs,\n\t\t\t\t   core_yyscan_t yyscanner)\n{\n\tFunctionParameter *lastd = (FunctionParameter *) llast(directargs);\n\tInteger    *ndirectargs;\n\n\t/* No restriction unless last direct arg is VARIADIC */\n\tif (lastd->mode == FUNC_PARAM_VARIADIC)\n\t{\n\t\tFunctionParameter *firsto = (FunctionParameter *) linitial(orderedargs);\n\n\t\t/*\n\t\t * We ignore the names, though the aggr_arg production allows them; it\n\t\t * doesn't allow default values, so those need not be checked.\n\t\t */\n\t\tif (list_length(orderedargs) != 1 ||\n\t\t\tfirsto->mode != FUNC_PARAM_VARIADIC ||\n\t\t\t!equal(lastd->argType, firsto->argType))\n\t\t\tereport(ERROR,\n\t\t\t\t\t(errcode(ERRCODE_FEATURE_NOT_SUPPORTED),\n\t\t\t\t\t errmsg(\"an ordered-set aggregate with a VARIADIC direct argument must have one VARIADIC aggregated argument of the same data type\"),\n\t\t\t\t\t parser_errposition(firsto->location)));\n\n\t\t/* OK, drop the duplicate VARIADIC argument from the internal form */\n\t\torderedargs = NIL;\n\t}\n\n\t/* don't merge into the next line, as list_concat changes directargs */\n\tndirectargs = makeInteger(list_length(directargs));\n\n\treturn list_make2(list_concat(directargs, orderedargs),\n\t\t\t\t\t  ndirectargs);\n}\n\n/* insertSelectOptions()\n * Insert ORDER BY, etc into an already-constructed SelectStmt.\n *\n * This routine is just to avoid duplicating code in SelectStmt productions.\n */\nstatic void\ninsertSelectOptions(SelectStmt *stmt,\n\t\t\t\t\tList *sortClause, List *lockingClause,\n\t\t\t\t\tSelectLimit *limitClause,\n\t\t\t\t\tWithClause *withClause,\n\t\t\t\t\tcore_yyscan_t yyscanner)\n{\n\tAssert(IsA(stmt, SelectStmt));\n\n\t/*\n\t * Tests here are to reject constructs like\n\t *\t(SELECT foo ORDER BY bar) ORDER BY baz\n\t */\n\tif (sortClause)\n\t{\n\t\tif (stmt->sortClause)\n\t\t\tereport(ERROR,\n\t\t\t\t\t(errcode(ERRCODE_SYNTAX_ERROR),\n\t\t\t\t\t errmsg(\"multiple ORDER BY clauses not allowed\"),\n\t\t\t\t\t parser_errposition(exprLocation((Node *) sortClause))));\n\t\tstmt->sortClause = sortClause;\n\t}\n\t/* We can handle multiple locking clauses, though */\n\tstmt->lockingClause = list_concat(stmt->lockingClause, lockingClause);\n\tif (limitClause && limitClause->limitOffset)\n\t{\n\t\tif (stmt->limitOffset)\n\t\t\tereport(ERROR,\n\t\t\t\t\t(errcode(ERRCODE_SYNTAX_ERROR),\n\t\t\t\t\t errmsg(\"multiple OFFSET clauses not allowed\"),\n\t\t\t\t\t parser_errposition(limitClause->offsetLoc)));\n\t\tstmt->limitOffset = limitClause->limitOffset;\n\t}\n\tif (limitClause && limitClause->limitCount)\n\t{\n\t\tif (stmt->limitCount)\n\t\t\tereport(ERROR,\n\t\t\t\t\t(errcode(ERRCODE_SYNTAX_ERROR),\n\t\t\t\t\t errmsg(\"multiple LIMIT clauses not allowed\"),\n\t\t\t\t\t parser_errposition(limitClause->countLoc)));\n\t\tstmt->limitCount = limitClause->limitCount;\n\t}\n\tif (limitClause)\n\t{\n\t\t/* If there was a conflict, we must have detected it above */\n\t\tAssert(!stmt->limitOption);\n\t\tif (!stmt->sortClause && limitClause->limitOption == LIMIT_OPTION_WITH_TIES)\n\t\t\tereport(ERROR,\n\t\t\t\t\t(errcode(ERRCODE_SYNTAX_ERROR),\n\t\t\t\t\t errmsg(\"WITH TIES cannot be specified without ORDER BY clause\"),\n\t\t\t\t\t parser_errposition(limitClause->optionLoc)));\n\t\tif (limitClause->limitOption == LIMIT_OPTION_WITH_TIES && stmt->lockingClause)\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tListCell   *lc;\n\n\t\t\tforeach(lc, stmt->lockingClause)\n\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\tLockingClause *lock = lfirst_node(LockingClause, lc);\n\n\t\t\t\tif (lock->waitPolicy == LockWaitSkip)\n\t\t\t\t\tereport(ERROR,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t(errcode(ERRCODE_SYNTAX_ERROR),\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t errmsg(\"%s and %s options cannot be used together\",\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\"SKIP LOCKED\", \"WITH TIES\"),\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t parser_errposition(limitClause->optionLoc)));\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t\tstmt->limitOption = limitClause->limitOption;\n\t}\n\tif (withClause)\n\t{\n\t\tif (stmt->withClause)\n\t\t\tereport(ERROR,\n\t\t\t\t\t(errcode(ERRCODE_SYNTAX_ERROR),\n\t\t\t\t\t errmsg(\"multiple WITH clauses not allowed\"),\n\t\t\t\t\t parser_errposition(exprLocation((Node *) withClause))));\n\t\tstmt->withClause = withClause;\n\t}\n}\n\nstatic Node *\nmakeSetOp(SetOperation op, bool all, Node *larg, Node *rarg)\n{\n\tSelectStmt *n = makeNode(SelectStmt);\n\n\tn->op = op;\n\tn->all = all;\n\tn->larg = (SelectStmt *) larg;\n\tn->rarg = (SelectStmt *) rarg;\n\treturn (Node *) n;\n}\n\n/* SystemFuncName()\n * Build a properly-qualified reference to a built-in function.\n */\nList *\nSystemFuncName(char *name)\n{\n\treturn list_make2(makeString(\"pg_catalog\"), makeString(name));\n}\n\n/* SystemTypeName()\n * Build a properly-qualified reference to a built-in type.\n *\n * typmod is defaulted, but may be changed afterwards by caller.\n * Likewise for the location.\n */\nTypeName *\nSystemTypeName(char *name)\n{\n\treturn makeTypeNameFromNameList(list_make2(makeString(\"pg_catalog\"),\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t   makeString(name)));\n}\n\n/* doNegate()\n * Handle negation of a numeric constant.\n *\n * Formerly, we did this here because the optimizer couldn't cope with\n * indexquals that looked like \"var = -4\" --- it wants \"var = const\"\n * and a unary minus operator applied to a constant didn't qualify.\n * As of Postgres 7.0, that problem doesn't exist anymore because there\n * is a constant-subexpression simplifier in the optimizer.  However,\n * there's still a good reason for doing this here, which is that we can\n * postpone committing to a particular internal representation for simple\n * negative constants.\tIt's better to leave \"-123.456\" in string form\n * until we know what the desired type is.\n */\nstatic Node *\ndoNegate(Node *n, int location)\n{\n\tif (IsA(n, A_Const))\n\t{\n\t\tA_Const    *con = (A_Const *) n;\n\n\t\t/* report the constant's location as that of the '-' sign */\n\t\tcon->location = location;\n\n\t\tif (IsA(&con->val, Integer))\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tcon->val.ival.ival = -con->val.ival.ival;\n\t\t\treturn n;\n\t\t}\n\t\tif (IsA(&con->val, Float))\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tdoNegateFloat(&con->val.fval);\n\t\t\treturn n;\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn (Node *) makeSimpleA_Expr(AEXPR_OP, \"-\", NULL, n, location);\n}\n\nstatic void\ndoNegateFloat(Float *v)\n{\n\tchar\t   *oldval = v->fval;\n\n\tif (*oldval == '+')\n\t\toldval++;\n\tif (*oldval == '-')\n\t\tv->fval = oldval + 1;\t/* just strip the '-' */\n\telse\n\t\tv->fval = psprintf(\"-%s\", oldval);\n}\n\nstatic Node *\nmakeAndExpr(Node *lexpr, Node *rexpr, int location)\n{\n\t/* Flatten \"a AND b AND c ...\" to a single BoolExpr on sight */\n\tif (IsA(lexpr, BoolExpr))\n\t{\n\t\tBoolExpr   *blexpr = (BoolExpr *) lexpr;\n\n\t\tif (blexpr->boolop == AND_EXPR)\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tblexpr->args = lappend(blexpr->args, rexpr);\n\t\t\treturn (Node *) blexpr;\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn (Node *) makeBoolExpr(AND_EXPR, list_make2(lexpr, rexpr), location);\n}\n\nstatic Node *\nmakeOrExpr(Node *lexpr, Node *rexpr, int location)\n{\n\t/* Flatten \"a OR b OR c ...\" to a single BoolExpr on sight */\n\tif (IsA(lexpr, BoolExpr))\n\t{\n\t\tBoolExpr   *blexpr = (BoolExpr *) lexpr;\n\n\t\tif (blexpr->boolop == OR_EXPR)\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tblexpr->args = lappend(blexpr->args, rexpr);\n\t\t\treturn (Node *) blexpr;\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn (Node *) makeBoolExpr(OR_EXPR, list_make2(lexpr, rexpr), location);\n}\n\nstatic Node *\nmakeNotExpr(Node *expr, int location)\n{\n\treturn (Node *) makeBoolExpr(NOT_EXPR, list_make1(expr), location);\n}\n\nstatic Node *\nmakeAArrayExpr(List *elements, int location, int location_end)\n{\n\tA_ArrayExpr *n = makeNode(A_ArrayExpr);\n\n\tn->elements = elements;\n\tn->location = location;\n\tn->list_start = location;\n\tn->list_end = location_end;\n\treturn (Node *) n;\n}\n\nstatic Node *\nmakeSQLValueFunction(SQLValueFunctionOp op, int32 typmod, int location)\n{\n\tSQLValueFunction *svf = makeNode(SQLValueFunction);\n\n\tsvf->op = op;\n\t/* svf->type will be filled during parse analysis */\n\tsvf->typmod = typmod;\n\tsvf->location = location;\n\treturn (Node *) svf;\n}\n\nstatic Node *\nmakeXmlExpr(XmlExprOp op, char *name, List *named_args, List *args,\n\t\t\tint location)\n{\n\tXmlExpr    *x = makeNode(XmlExpr);\n\n\tx->op = op;\n\tx->name = name;\n\n\t/*\n\t * named_args is a list of ResTarget; it'll be split apart into separate\n\t * expression and name lists in transformXmlExpr().\n\t */\n\tx->named_args = named_args;\n\tx->arg_names = NIL;\n\tx->args = args;\n\t/* xmloption, if relevant, must be filled in by caller */\n\t/* type and typmod will be filled in during parse analysis */\n\tx->type = InvalidOid;\t\t/* marks the node as not analyzed */\n\tx->location = location;\n\treturn (Node *) x;\n}\n\n/*\n * Merge the input and output parameters of a table function.\n */\nstatic List *\nmergeTableFuncParameters(List *func_args, List *columns, core_yyscan_t yyscanner)\n{\n\tListCell   *lc;\n\n\t/* Explicit OUT and INOUT parameters shouldn't be used in this syntax */\n\tforeach(lc, func_args)\n\t{\n\t\tFunctionParameter *p = (FunctionParameter *) lfirst(lc);\n\n\t\tif (p->mode != FUNC_PARAM_DEFAULT &&\n\t\t\tp->mode != FUNC_PARAM_IN &&\n\t\t\tp->mode != FUNC_PARAM_VARIADIC)\n\t\t\tereport(ERROR,\n\t\t\t\t\t(errcode(ERRCODE_SYNTAX_ERROR),\n\t\t\t\t\t errmsg(\"OUT and INOUT arguments aren't allowed in TABLE functions\"),\n\t\t\t\t\t parser_errposition(p->location)));\n\t}\n\n\treturn list_concat(func_args, columns);\n}\n\n/*\n * Determine return type of a TABLE function.  A single result column\n * returns setof that column's type; otherwise return setof record.\n */\nstatic TypeName *\nTableFuncTypeName(List *columns)\n{\n\tTypeName   *result;\n\n\tif (list_length(columns) == 1)\n\t{\n\t\tFunctionParameter *p = (FunctionParameter *) linitial(columns);\n\n\t\tresult = copyObject(p->argType);\n\t}\n\telse\n\t\tresult = SystemTypeName(\"record\");\n\n\tresult->setof = true;\n\n\treturn result;\n}\n\n/*\n * Convert a list of (dotted) names to a RangeVar (like\n * makeRangeVarFromNameList, but with position support). strVal(linitial(names));\n\t\t\tr->schemaname = strVal(lsecond(names));\n\t\t\tr->relname = strVal(lthird(names));\n\t\t\tbreak;\n\t\tdefault:\n\t\t\tereport(ERROR,\n\t\t\t\t\t(errcode(ERRCODE_SYNTAX_ERROR),\n\t\t\t\t\t errmsg(\"improper qualified name (too many dotted names): r;\n}\n\n/*\n * Convert a relation_name with name and namelist to a RangeVar using\n * makeRangeVar.\n */\nstatic RangeVar *\nmakeRangeVarFromQualifiedName(char *name, List *namelist, int clauses in a ColQualList */\nstatic void\nSplitColQualList(List *qualList,\n\t\t\t\t List **constraintList, CollateClause **collClause,\n\t\t\t\t core_yyscan_t yyscanner)\n{\n\tListCell   *cell;\n\n\t*collClause = NULL;\n\tforeach(cell, qualList)\n\t{\n\t\tNode\t   *n = (Node *) lfirst(cell);\n\n\t\tif (IsA(n, Constraint))\n\t\t{\n\t\t\t/* keep it in list Grammar Extension (src/backend/parser/gram.y)\n**Change**: Added `HEADER_P MATCH` production rule to the `copy_opt_item` grammar rule (lines 3499-3502)\n\n```bison\n| HEADER_P MATCH\n{\n    $$ = makeDefElem(\"header\", (Node *) makeString(\"match\"), @1);\n}\n```\n\nThis enables support for:\n- Old syntax: `COPY table FROM file HEADER MATCH`\n- New syntax: `COPY table FROM file WITH (HEADER MATCH)` (already supported via generic options)\n\n### 2. Data Structures (src/include/commands/copy.h)\nAlready in place:\n```c\ntypedef enum CopyHeaderChoice\n{\n    COPY_HEADER_FALSE = 0,\n    COPY_HEADER_TRUE,\n    COPY_HEADER_MATCH,  /* ← New enum value */\n} CopyHeaderChoice;\n```\n\n### 3. Option Processing (src/backend/commands/copy.c)\nAlready implemented in `defGetCopyHeaderChoice()`:\n- Parses \"match\" string → `COPY_HEADER_MATCH` enum value\n- Validates HEADER MATCH is only for COPY FROM (errors for COPY TO)\n- Accepts \"true\", \"false\", \"on\", \"off\", \"match\" values\n\n### 4. Header Validation (src/backend/commands/copyfromparse.c)\nAlready implemented in `NextCopyFromRawFieldsInternal()`:\n- Reads first line of CSV as column headers\n- Compares each header field with table column names\n- Case-sensitive comparison using `namestrcmp()`\n- Validates:\n  - Same number of fields as expected columns\n  - Column names match exactly (case-sensitive)\n  - No null values in header fields\n\nError messages include:\n- \"wrong number of fields in header line: got X, expected Y\"\n- \"column name mismatch in header line field N: got \"X\", expected \"Y\"\"\n\n### 5. Regression Tests\nAlready exist in `src/test/regress/sql/copy.sql` (lines 237-305):\n\n**Test Cases**:\n1. ✅ Successful HEADER MATCH with matching columns\n2. ✅ HEADER MATCH with reordered columns\n3. ✅ HEADER MATCH with dropped columns\n4. ✅ Error: HEADER MATCH with wrong column names\n5. ✅ Error: HEADER MATCH with wrong column count\n6. ✅ Error: HEADER MATCH with null values in header\n7. ✅ Error: HEADER MATCH with COPY TO (not allowed)\n8. ✅ Error: Invalid header option value\n\n**Expected Output** in `src/test/regress/expected/copy.out`:\n- Proper error messages with context (line numbers)\n- Correct data loaded for successful cases\n\n## Feature Completeness\n\n### Requirement Checklist:\n✅ **Grammar Extended**\n   - HEADER_P MATCH rule added to copy_opt_item\n   - Supports both old and new COPY syntax\n\n✅ **Option Processing**\n   - HEADER MATCH option parses correctly\n   - Flag is stored in CopyFormatOptions.header_line as COPY_HEADER_MATCH enum\n   - Validated only for COPY FROM (error for COPY TO)\n   - Validated only for CSV format (error for BINARY)\n\n✅ **Header Validation**\n   - Reads CSV header line after opening file\n   - Compares column names (case-sensitive)\n   - Validates column count matches\n   - Descriptive error messages for mismatches\n\n✅ **Regression Tests**\n   - Tests for matching headers (success case)\n   - Tests for mismatched headers (error cases)\n   - Tests for wrong column count\n   - Tests for edge cases (dropped columns, reordering)\n\n## Code Files Modified\n\n### New Changes:\n- `src/backend/parser/gram.y` - Added HEADER_P MATCH grammar rule\n\n### Already Implemented (Verified):\n- `src/include/commands/copy.h` - Expected test output\n\n## Testing\n\nThe feature includes comprehensive regression tests that verify:\n1. The feature validates that CSV file header row column names match the target table's column names.\n\n### What Was Done\n\n#### 1. **Grammar Extension** (src/backend/parser/gram.y)\nAdded a new grammar rule to support the `HEADER MATCH` syntax:\n```sql\nCOPY table FROM file HEADER MATCH\nCOPY table FROM file WITH (HEADER MATCH)\n```\n\nThe new rule creates a DefElem with the string value \"match\" which is parsed by the existing `defGetCopyHeaderChoice()` function.\n\n#### 2. **Verified Existing Implementation**\nThe following components were already implemented and verified:\n\n- **Data Structures** (copy.h): `CopyHeaderChoice` enum with `COPY_HEADER_MATCH` value\n- **Option Processing** (copy.c): `defGetCopyHeaderChoice()` function that:\n  -
